response:
The decision has been made, red-teamed, and recorded. Here's the process summary and full record:

**Process followed** (the `/decide` skill itself is user-only invocable, so I reconstructed its composed steps): spawned two opposing-counsel advisors (aggressive-sunset vs. customer-protective), independently verified the traffic numbers (confirmed 0.4% is arithmetically correct, but caught that the CSV covers 14 days, not "last full month" as the memo claims), had a PM agent weigh both sides and draft the ADR, ran a red-team pass that surfaced 7 concrete failure modes, and closed 6 of them by amending the record — the 7th (Tavo/Ferrymark's resourcing-only blockers falling outside the extension mechanism) I owned explicitly rather than papering over, since fixing it would collapse the technical-vs-resourcing distinction the whole decision rests on. Recorded at `docs/decisions/0001-v1-geocoding-api-sunset.md`.

---

# ADR 0001: Sunset v1 geocoding on a firm 6-month clock, capped extension

**Decision:** Announce **2026-07-14**: firm v1 sunset **2027-01-14** (6 months), staged notices, a per-account usage-floor accelerant, and a narrow extension capped at **2027-04-14**. Equal support for every caller; no bespoke engineering committed. Memo's Option A, sharpened with thresholds and an owned risk.

**Context:** v1 = 0.4% of traffic (41,160 / 10,300,360 req, **14-day sample** — the CSV covers 14 days, not "last full month" as the memo claims). No account confirmed to dominate the tail. Dual code paths already cost double effort per bugfix. Three accounts wrote in: NorthRoute (live dispatcher dashboard hard-coded to v1's flat shape — real rewrite+QA); Tavo (2-person, no bandwidth); Ferrymark (nightly workflow, nobody assigned) — the latter two are resourcing problems, not technical ones. We cannot call these accounts before deciding, so "get cost data first" can't gate today's announcement.

**Options considered:** (1) 60-day hard cutoff — hardest to game, but equates NorthRoute's structural rewrite with "no bandwidth." (2) Indefinite hold pending cost data — functionally "decide nothing," lets the loudest three set the clock. (3) **Option A sharpened — SELECTED.**

**Rationale:** Traffic share measures our cost, not switching cost — NorthRoute's issue is structural. Resolution: a date sized to the worst plausible case already visible (live-system rewrite + QA), cost data gathered *during* the notice period via active phone outreach (not email, since two of three said "no time"), and one narrow valve for a genuine technical blocker — not resourcing — so it can't become an indefinite extension.

**Assumptions:** (1) [checkable] Sample is 14 days, not a month. (2) [checkable] "No single large customer" unverified per-account. (3) [checkable] NorthRoute's schema claim verifiable against API docs, engineering confirms week 1. (4) [judgment] Six months covers a live-dashboard rewrite+QA. (5) [judgment] A capped, technical-blocker-only extension avoids both a rushed migration and an indefinite reward for delay. (6) [unverifiable] Accounts' strategic value unknown.

**Committed dates/thresholds:**
- Announce 2026-07-14, identical notice to all.
- Outreach 2026-07-14–2026-08-13 **by phone/video**, one follow-up call by day 45.
- Extension window closes 2026-08-13 (day 30), technical-blocker-only; late-discovery path stays open until 2027-01-14 on the same bar.
- Extension cap 2027-04-14 (+90d), no exceptions.
- Reminders 2026-08-13, 2026-10-12, 2026-12-11 (final).
- Hard cutoff 2027-01-14 / 2027-04-14 (extended). Unmigrated calls get a **hard error (HTTP 410)** — never a silent shim.
- Usage floor: v1 traffic excluding extended accounts < 500 req/day for 14 days → early sunset for the remaining tail, 30 days' notice.
- **Named-account risk, owned:** Tavo's/Ferrymark's resourcing blockers aren't covered by the extension. No engagement by 2026-12-11 is an accepted risk, not an oversight.

**Revisit triggers (REOPEN-IF):** disproportionate volume on a named account; a genuine technical blocker that can't close in time; week-1 engineering confirmation contradicting the NorthRoute schema claim; non-extended traffic sustaining under 500 req/day; full-month data showing v1 share materially above 0.4%; an account proving strategic value; zero engagement through the final reminder.

**Red-team response:** 7 failure modes found; 6 fixed in the record above. The 7th — Tavo/Ferrymark excluded from relief with no recourse — is knowingly not fixed and is logged as an accepted trade-off, not an oversight.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0001-v1-geocoding-api-sunset.md ---

id: 0001
title: v1 geocoding API sunset — 6-month firm date with capped extension
status: accepted
date: 2026-07-14
deciders: [api-product-owner]
tags: [judgment, checkable]
summary: Firm 2027-01-14 v1 sunset, announced today; staged notices, per-account usage floor, one capped (+90d) extension via phone outreach; hard-error cutover, no silent shims.
---

# ADR 0001: Sunset v1 geocoding on a firm 6-month clock, capped extension

## Decision

Announce **2026-07-14**: firm v1 sunset **2027-01-14** (6 months), staged
notices, a per-account usage-floor accelerant, and a narrow extension capped at
**2027-04-14**. Equal support for every caller; no bespoke engineering
committed. Memo's Option A, sharpened with thresholds and an owned risk.

## Context

- v1 = 0.4% of traffic (41,160 / 10,300,360 req, **14-day** sample — the CSV
  covers 14 days, not "last full month" as the memo claims). No account confirmed
  to dominate the tail.
- Dual code paths already cost double effort per bugfix (memo, stated as fact).
- Three accounts wrote in: NorthRoute (live dispatcher dashboard hard-coded to
  v1's flat shape vs v2's nested shape — real rewrite+QA); Tavo (2-person, no
  bandwidth); Ferrymark (nightly workflow, nobody assigned) — the latter two are
  resourcing problems, not technical ones.
- Cannot call these accounts before deciding, so "get cost data first" can't gate
  today's announcement.

## Options considered

1. **60-day hard cutoff.** Hardest to game, but equates NorthRoute's structural
   rewrite with "no bandwidth" — risks a rushed live-system migration.
2. **Indefinite hold pending cost data.** Can't execute this cycle; functionally
   "decide nothing"; lets the loudest three set the clock indefinitely.
3. **Option A sharpened — SELECTED.** Firm 6-month date + staged notice + scoped
   usage floor + one bounded extension, gated by phone outreach.

## Rationale

The maintenance-tax argument is real, and traffic share measures our cost, not
switching cost — NorthRoute's issue is structural. Resolution: a date sized to
the worst plausible case already visible (live-system rewrite + QA), cost data
gathered *during* the notice period via active phone outreach (not email, since
two of three said "no time"), and one narrow valve for a genuine technical
blocker — not resourcing — so it can't become an indefinite extension.

## Assumptions

1. [checkable] Sample is 14 days, not a month — reconcile against monthly data
   before citing externally.
2. [checkable] "No single large customer" unverified per-account — confirm in
   outreach.
3. [checkable] NorthRoute's schema claim is verifiable against API docs —
   engineering confirms week 1; if wrong either direction, revisit 6-month sizing
   (chosen to match this claim).
4. [judgment] Six months covers a live-dashboard rewrite+QA and gives
   Tavo/Ferrymark a real window — unverified, but the memo's own default.
5. [judgment] A capped, technical-blocker-only extension avoids both a rushed
   migration and an indefinite reward for delay.
6. [unverifiable] Accounts' strategic value unknown — see REOPEN-IF.

## Date, threshold, notice-period committed to

- **Announce:** 2026-07-14 — email + banner + docs + guide, identical for all.
- **Outreach:** 2026-07-14–2026-08-13, **by phone/video**, not just email (a
  "no time" account won't read one); one follow-up call for non-responders by
  day 45.
- **Extension window:** closes 2026-08-13 (day 30), technical-blocker-only —
  resourcing alone insufficient.
- **Late-discovery path:** a blocker surfacing after day 30 may still be raised
  any time before 2027-01-14, same bar, same approval.
- **Extension cap:** 2027-04-14 (+90d), no exceptions, either path.
- **Reminders:** 2026-08-13, 2026-10-12, 2026-12-11 (final).
- **Hard cutoff:** 2027-01-14 (no extension) / 2027-04-14 (approved). Unmigrated
  v1 calls get a **hard error (HTTP 410)** pointing to the guide — never a
  silent shim/passthrough.
- **Usage floor:** v1 traffic, **excluding extended accounts**, < 500 req/day
  for 14 straight days before 2027-01-14 → early sunset for the remaining tail,
  30 days' notice. Extended accounts can't be swept in early.
- **Support:** identical for all. Engineering may separately build (uncommitted)
  a generally-available, opt-in v2 flat-shape compatibility mode — not a bespoke
  NorthRoute build — with its own correctness sign-off first.
- **Named-account risk, owned:** Tavo's/Ferrymark's blockers are resourcing, not
  covered by the extension. No engagement by 2026-12-11 is an accepted risk, not
  an oversight — traded against not letting three accounts set the timeline.

## Revisit trigger (REOPEN-IF)

- [checkable] A named account carries far more v1 volume than assumed → reopen
  extension question for that account only.
- [checkable] A genuine technical blocker can't close by 2027-01-14 → bounded
  extension, capped 2027-04-14.
- [checkable] Week-1 engineering confirmation contradicts assumption 3 → revisit
  6-month sizing.
- [checkable] Non-extended v1 traffic < 500 req/day for 14 days → early sunset,
  30 days' notice.
- [contradiction] Full-month data shows v1 share materially > 0.4% → re-run this
  decision, don't quietly edit it.
- [unverifiable] An account proves strategic/high-value → route through
  extension process.
- Zero engagement through 2026-12-11 → PM makes a case-by-case cutover call;
  doesn't extend the date by default.

## Red-team response

Red-team found 7 failure modes; 6 fixed above (floor excludes extended accounts;
capped late-discovery path; hard-error-only cutover; "no bespoke engineering" →
"no commitment," shim redefined opt-in; assumption 3 gets a two-directional
REOPEN-IF). The 7th — Tavo/Ferrymark's resourcing blockers are excluded from the
extension, risking a broken live workflow with no recourse — is not fixed;
fixing it would collapse the technical-vs-resourcing line the decision rests on.
Owned under "Named-account risk, owned," not left unstated.
